name: coding-standards
tagline: Baseline cross-project coding conventions (naming, immutability, readability, code smells) with paired good/bad code examples.
verdict: Extensive, well-exemplified conventions doc, but its own Scope Boundaries section explicitly excludes React and API-design content, then the body immediately delivers full React Best Practices and API Design Standards sections - a direct self-contradiction. Also unversioned, and the bulky framework catalogs should be split into reference files rather than.

highlights.summary: Description states the topic (naming, readability, immutability, review) but doesn't phrase an explicit usage trigger for itself.
Scope Boundaries says 'Do not use this skill as primary source for React composition... or API design', yet the body has full React Best Practices and API Design Standards sections.
549-line SKILL.md inlines full React, API-design, file-organization, testing, and performance catalogs that are only sometimes needed, with no reference-file split.
